description:
Account Data Deletion with Selective Purge & Confirmation Gate

Implement a self-service data deletion flow that allows authenticated users to permanently and irreversibly remove some or all of their personal data from the system. The feature must prevent accidental deletion through an explicit text-confirmation gate and support granular selection of data categories to delete.

---

Requirements:

UI & Flow
- Add a "Delete Account Data" section in the user's Settings or Profile page, placed in a visually distinct Danger Zone area
- Present a checklist of deletable data categories (all checked by default), allowing the user to deselect categories they wish to retain:
  - Inbox / Emails (aggregated messages, attachments, embeddings)
  - Kanban Boards, Columns & Cards (including labels, tags, comments, attachments)
  - Meeting Transcripts & Intelligence (Krisp webhooks, key points, action items, speakers)
  - Open Brain Memories (thought captures, vector embeddings, metadata)
  - Pages / Notes (markdown content)
  - Action Items (all lifecycle states — open, in_progress, completed, cancelled)
  - Calendar Events
  - Audit Logs & Activity History
- Surface a clear summary line before confirmation, e.g. "You are about to permanently delete: Inbox, Kanban, Meetings (3 categories selected)"
- Require the user to type the exact phrase DELETE MY DATA (case-insensitive acceptable, but must match) into a text input field before the destructive action is enabled
- Confirm button must remain disabled until the typed phrase matches exactly
- On submit, show an inline loading state and prevent duplicate submissions

Backend & Data Integrity
- Implement a single API endpoint (e.g., POST /api/account/delete-data) that accepts the list of selected categories and the confirmation phrase, and re-validates the phrase server-side before executing
- All deletions must cascade correctly — remove child records (embeddings, action items linked to meetings, cards within columns, etc.) before parent records to avoid foreign key violations
- Vector embeddings stored in pgvector must be explicitly deleted alongside their parent records
- Soft-deleted records (trash) must also be purged within the selected categories
- Wrap all deletions in a database transaction — if any category deletion fails, roll back the entire operation and return a descriptive error
- Log the deletion event to the audit trail before executing (record which categories, timestamp, user ID) so a pre-deletion record exists even if the user later deletes audit logs separately

Post-Deletion Behavior
- If the user deletes all categories (full wipe), invalidate their session and redirect to a confirmation screen with a message such as "Your data has been permanently deleted"
- If a partial deletion is performed, reload the affected views and surfaces to reflect the removed data without stale state
- Send a transactional confirmation email via Resend summarising which categories were deleted and at what time

Edge Cases & Constraints
- Deletion must be scoped strictly to the authenticated user's own data — no cross-user access possible at the query level
- Handle the case where a category has no data gracefully (skip silently, do not surface as an error)
- If a third-party sync (Gmail push, Microsoft Graph subscription) is active, unsubscribe from those webhooks before deleting the associated email data to prevent orphaned subscriptions
- Rate-limit the endpoint to prevent abuse (e.g., max 3 deletion requests per user per hour)
- Do not expose the deletion endpoint to API key holders — require a valid NextAuth session only
title: Implement selective account data deletion with confirmation gate

summary:
## Summary: Account Data Deletion with Selective Purge & Confirmation Gate

### Changes Implemented
- **API Endpoint** (`POST /api/account/delete-data`): Accepts selected data categories and a confirmation phrase. Re-validates the phrase server-side (`DELETE MY DATA`, case-insensitive). Deletes data within a database transaction — if any deletion fails, the entire operation is rolled back. Includes per-user rate limiting (3 requests/hour). Requires a valid NextAuth session (no API key access). Sends a confirmation email via Resend after successful deletion.
- **Danger Zone UI** (`DeleteAccountDataClient.tsx`): Visually distinct red-bordered danger zone with 9 selectable data categories (all checked by default). Each category shows a description of affected data. Displays a summary line before confirmation (e.g., "You are about to permanently delete: Inbox / Emails, Kanban Boards (2 categories selected)"). Requires typing `DELETE MY DATA` to enable the delete button. Shows loading state and prevents duplicate submissions. On full wipe, signs the user out and redirects to login. On partial deletion, shows success with a reload button.
- **Account Page** (`app/(app)/admin/account/page.tsx`): Server component page following existing admin page patterns (uses `getRequiredUser()` for auth).
- **SideNav Integration**: Added "Account" nav item with user icon between Extensions and Trash in the sidebar navigation.

### Data Categories (9 total)
1. **Inbox / Emails** — emails, Gmail sync, Zoom chats, embeddings, smart labels, VIP contacts, newsletters, drafts, briefings, weekly reviews
2. **Kanban Boards** — boards → columns → cards → tags (cascade). Clears defaultBoardId/emailActionBoardId on user record
3. **Meeting Transcripts** — Krisp webhook key points (decisions/actionItems reference with SET NULL)
4. **Open Brain Memories** — thoughts with vector embeddings, chat sessions, thought links, reminders
5. **Pages / Notes** — workspaces → pages → blocks, database rows, page entries (cascade)
6. **Action Items** — all lifecycle states
7. **Calendar Events** — calendar events, sync state, Microsoft Graph subscriptions
8. **Decisions** — decision records
9. **Settings & Integrations** — OAuth tokens (Google, Outlook, Zoom), webhook secrets, outbound webhooks, Telegram bots, custom prompts, Zapier logs, extension downloads

### Files Modified
- `components/ui/SideNav.tsx` — Added AccountIcon component and "Account" nav item with route matching

### Files Created
- `app/api/account/delete-data/route.ts` — API endpoint with transaction, rate limiting, validation, email notification
- `app/(app)/admin/account/page.tsx` — Server component account settings page
- `app/(app)/admin/account/DeleteAccountDataClient.tsx` — Client component with category checklist, confirmation gate, loading states

### Notes for Developer
- All deletions run inside a single `db.transaction()` — if any category fails, everything rolls back
- Foreign key CASCADE handles child record deletion (e.g., deleting boards cascades to columns → cards → card_tags)
- Vector embeddings in `emails`, `brainThoughts`, and `zoomChatMessages` are deleted with their parent records via CASCADE
- Rate limit is in-memory (per-process) — sufficient for serverless but resets on cold start
- The confirmation email is non-blocking — a failure to send won't prevent the deletion from succeeding
- When all 9 categories are deleted (full wipe), the user is signed out after 3 seconds
- The endpoint only accepts NextAuth sessions, not API keys
- Pre-existing TypeScript errors in Stripe files are unrelated to this change
